title: The effect of speech masking on the human subcortical response to continuous speech
description: This dataset comprises auditory brainstem response (ABR) recordings from 25 normal-hearing participants exposed to continuous speech under varying masking conditions. Participants listened to peaky speech from one to five simultaneous talkers at different signal-to-noise ratios (clean, 0 dB, -3 dB, -6 dB SNR), as well as click trains, while EEG was recorded. The study investigates how speech masking affects subcortical neural encoding of naturally produced speech, utilizing a recently developed method for ABR extraction to continuous speech stimuli.
methods_description: EEG recordings were collected from 25 participants (ages 19-37 years, mean 23.4 ± 5.5 years) with normal hearing (audiometric thresholds ≤20 dB HL from 500-8000 Hz). Stimuli included randomized click trains (40 Hz average rate, 60 × 10 s trials) and peaky speech from male narrators at four SNR conditions (clean, 0 dB, -3 dB, -6 dB SNR corresponding to 1, 2, 3, and 5 talkers). All stimuli were presented at 65 dB SPL through ER-2 insert earphones at 48 kHz sampling rate in a sound-isolating booth. EEG was recorded in Brain Products format and processed using custom Python scripts with expyfun for stimulus control.
